tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

reply_to_id.description: Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":58545,"title":"[ServiceNetworking] Docs: Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking README gaps","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking` **Service directory:** `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/` **Triggered by:** PR #58483 (commit 60f88fae6) by `@ArcturusZhang` ## What Changed Introduced new provisioning library `Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking` (1.0.0-beta.1) with support for four Microsoft.ServiceNetworking resources: `TrafficController`, `TrafficControllerFrontend`, `TrafficControllerAssociation`, and `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`. ## Gaps Found - **Empty "Authenticate the client" section** — The README has the section heading but no content; provisioning libraries don't require an `AzureCredential` in the same way but should note the `azd` / `AzureDeveloperCliCredential` usage pattern - **Empty "Next steps" section** — No links to related samples, Azure documentation for Application Gateway for Containers, or the `Azure.Provisioning` README - **`ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` is undocumented** — The CHANGELOG lists this resource but the README Examples section has no example or mention of it; a snippet showing how to create and attach a security policy to a traffic controller is missing 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/README.md` (edit) 1. **Fill in "Authenticate the client" section** — Replace the empty body under `### Authenticate the Client` with content similar to the pattern used in other provisioning packages (e.g., `sdk/storage/Azure.Provisioning.Storage/README.md`): ```markdown ### Authenticate the Client This library provisions Azure infrastructure using [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) Authentication is handled automatically by `azd` during deployment. For local development or CI/CD, you can use `AzureDeveloperCliCredential` or any other credential from the [`Azure.Identity`](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Azure.Identity) package. ``` 2. **Fill in "Next steps" section** — Replace the empty body under `## Next steps` with useful links: ```markdown ## Next steps - [Azure Application Gateway for Containers documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - [Azure.Provisioning overview](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md) - [Azure Developer CLI (`azd`) documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) ``` 3. **Add an `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` example** — After the existing `### Create a Traffic Controller` example, add: ```markdown ### Create a Security Policy This example demonstrates how to create an `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` and associate it with a Traffic Controller. ``` ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets **File to create:** `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/tests/BasicServiceNetworkingTests.cs` (edit — add a new test method) Add the following method to the existing `BasicServiceNetworkingTests` class, below `CreateTrafficController()`: ```csharp internal static Trycep CreateSecurityPolicyTest() { return new Trycep().Define( ctx => { #region Snippet:ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy Infrastructure infra = new(); TrafficController controller = new(nameof(controller), TrafficController.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01) { Tags = { ["environment"] = "test" }, }; infra.Add(controller); ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy policy = new(nameof(policy), ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01) { Parent = controller, }; infra.Add(policy); #endregion return infra; }); } [Test] public async Task CreateSecurityPolicy() { await using Trycep test = CreateSecurityPolicyTest(); // Expected Bicep output verified by test runner test.Compare( """ `@description`('The location for the resource(s) to be deployed.') param location string = resourceGroup().location resource controller 'Microsoft.ServiceNetworking/trafficControllers@2025-01-01' = { name: take('controller-\$\{uniqueString(resourceGroup().id)}', 24) location: location } resource policy 'Microsoft.ServiceNetworking/trafficControllers/securityPolicies@2025-01-01' = { name: take('policy-\$\{uniqueString(resourceGroup().id)}', 24) location: location parent: controller } """); } ``` > **Note:** Verify the exact Bicep resource type path for `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` by inspecting `src/Generated/ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy.cs` and its `ResourceVersions` inner class, then adjust the `test.Compare(...)` expected string accordingly. **Snippet name:** `ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy` **README location:** After the closing ` ``` ` of the `ServiceNetworkingBasic` snippet block, under `## Examples` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/README.md` currently has these sections: | Section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present | | Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present | | Getting started → Authenticate the client | ❌ Empty — needs content | | Key concepts | ✅ Present | | Examples | ⚠️ Partial — missing security policy example | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | | Next steps | ❌ Empty — needs links | | Contributing | ✅ Present | ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 servicenetworking` — injects `#region Snippet:` blocks from test files into README placeholders; check `git diff` afterwards to confirm README was updated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 servicenetworking` — regenerates public API listing files (only needed if public types changed) 5. `dotnet format sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > [!WARNING] > > ⚠️ Firewall blocked 1 domain > > The following domain was blocked by the firewall during workflow execution: > > - `centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com` > > To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter: > > ```yaml > network: > allowed: > - defaults > - "centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com" > ``` > > See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information. > > > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24815635172/agentic_workflow) · ● 737.3K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58545","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-23T03:56:14Z","updated_at":"2026-04-23T03:56:14Z"}

infra.Add(policy); #endregion return infra; }); } [Test] public async Task CreateSecurityPolicy() { await using Trycep test = CreateSecurityPolicyTest(); // Expected Bicep output verified by test runner test.Compare( """ `@description`('The location for the resource(s) to be deployed.') param location string = resourceGroup().location resource controller 'Microsoft.ServiceNetworking/trafficControllers@2025-01-01' = { name: take('controller-\$\{uniqueString(resourceGroup().id)}', 24) location: location } resource policy 'Microsoft.ServiceNetworking/trafficControllers/securityPolicies@2025-01-01' = { name: take('policy-\$\{uniqueString(resourceGroup().id)}', 24) location: location parent: controller } """); {"total_count":1,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":3292095671,"number":51705,"state":"closed","state_reason":"completed","locked":true,"title":"CosmosDBAccountCapability to implement IEquatable<CosmosDBAccountCapability>","body":"## Background
This is a "sub-issue" to one raised in the Aspire repo: [Upgrading to 9.4 causes duplicate capabilities in Cosmos DB and fails deployment](https://github.com/dotnet/aspire/issues/10808).

## Request
It would be great if `CosmosDBAccountCapability` could implement `IEquatable<CosmosDBAccountCapability>`. It would make the "upserting" of CosmosDB capabilities within the Aspire framework much easier if it were simple to compare 2 `CosmosDBAccountCapability` for equality based on their `Name` property alone.","author_association":"NONE","user":{"login":"goodcoffeecode","id":1698190,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE2OTgxOTA=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1698190?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/goodcoffeecode","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/goodcoffeecode/subscriptions"},"labels":[{"id":1178448111,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Mgmt","name":"Mgmt","color":"ffeb77","description":"This issue is related to a management package.","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMTc4NDQ4MTEx"},{"id":1353316183,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/customer-reported","name":"customer-reported","color":"3800e0","description":"Issues that are reported by GitHub users external to the Azure organization.","default":false,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMzUzMzE2MTgz"},{"id":1369687945,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/question","name":"question","color":"eaa875","description":"The issue doesn't require a change to the product in order to be resolved. ## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage — Needs Manual Review

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Decision</summary>

- **Candidate labels considered:**
  - `Provisioning` (category, color #ffeb77) + `ServiceNetworking` (service, color #e99695): Category is clearly applicable — package `Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking` maps to category "Provisioning" per the `Azure.Provisioning.*` naming pattern. However, the service label `ServiceNetworking` does not exist in this repository (confirmed via label lookup).
  - `Provisioning` + `Network - Application Gateway` (color #e99695): The issue mentions `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`, which relates to the Application Gateway for Containers product, but `%Network - Application Gateway` is associated with management-plane networking packages, not the provisioning SDK directory. This pairing is not a confident match.
- **Confidence blockers:** No existing service label (color #e99695) for `ServiceNetworking` was found in the repository. The confidence criterion requiring "exactly one service label" could not be satisfied. No qualifying reference issues (with exactly 1 category + 1 service + `customer-reported` + `issue-addressed`) were found to guide label selection.
- **Outcome:** Applied `needs-triage` — could not confidently predict labels

</details>
